title: Oscars 2026: Best Original Screenplay Winner Predictions | Polymarket
text: On Polymarket, the prediction market for 2026 Oscars Best Original Screenplay Winner showed strong consensus around "Sinners" by Ryan Coogler. Market Data: "Sinners" traded at 95% probability on the prediction market, indicating market participants' high confidence in the screenplay winning. With $413.2K traded on the "Oscars 2026: Best Original Screenplay Winner" market, these prices aggregated the collective knowledge and conviction of thousands of participants. Market Predictions: The next closest outcome was "Sentimental Value" at only 2%, with all other nominees having minimal odds. Analysis & Background: "Sinners" had already demonstrated dominance by winning awards at the Alliance of Women Film Journalists, Astra Film Awards, and Critics' Choice Awards. The film marked a potential historic achievement with the second Black writing winner with Ryan Coogler, following Jordan Peele's win for "Get Out" (2017). Coogler would be only the third Black person nominated in the same year for producing, directing, and screenwriting. "Sinners" had continually won awards for Best Original Screenplay, Best Casting, and Best Original Score, establishing its dominance in the category across multiple industry awards bodies.
